title: The New PM, Claire Vo | Compile 26
summary: Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, discusses how product management is evolving as code becomes abundant while market opportunities remain scarce. She argues that constraints have shifted from building capability to finding market fit, and that modern PMs must focus on discovering novel ideas and validating demand rather than managing engineering capacity.
writeup: A talk from Cursor's Compile 26 in which Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, makes the case that product management is shifting as code becomes abundant and market opportunities stay scarce. The argument is that the binding constraint has moved from building capability to finding market fit, which reframes the PM role. The concrete takeaway: modern PMs should concentrate on discovering novel ideas and validating demand rather than managing engineering capacity.
